hints: This invention was patented in 1955 after years of rejected applications.
It consists of two strips with hooks and loops that bond when pressed together.
Its name comes from combining the French words for velvet and hook.
This ruler's intelligence and linguistic abilities were as renowned as their appearance.
They were the last active pharaoh of ancient Egypt.
Their romantic relationships with Julius Caesar and Mark Antony are legendary.
This franchise was created by Satoshi Tajiri, inspired by his childhood hobby of collecting insects.
Its slogan encourages you to catch them all.
Pikachu is its most recognizable character.
This dish originated as a way for Alpine villagers to use hardened cheese and stale bread during winter.
The Swiss version typically features melted cheese or chocolate.
Long forks are traditionally used to prevent double-dipping.
This sport uses electronic scoring systems to detect valid touches during bouts.
Competitors wear protective masks and specialized weapons.
The three weapon types are foil, épée, and sabre.
This writing system was deciphered by Jean-François Champollion.
The Rosetta Stone was crucial to understanding it.
It was used extensively in ancient Egyptian tombs and temples.
This place features elaborate facades carved directly into sandstone cliff faces.
It was the capital of an ancient Nabataean kingdom.
Its Treasury building appeared in Indiana Jones and the Last Crusade.
This discovery came from observing contamination in laboratory cultures that created bacteria-free zones.
Fleming noticed it killed bacteria in 1928.
It became the world's first widely used antibiotic.
This person was placed under house arrest for the final years of their life.
They made significant improvements to the telescope.
They discovered four moons orbiting Jupiter.
This band recorded most of their albums at EMI Studios on Abbey Road.
They triggered a cultural phenomenon called mania named after them.
Their members were John, Paul, George, and Ringo.
This dish was traditionally cooked by field workers in Valencia over wood fires during lunch breaks.
The Valencian version traditionally includes rabbit and beans.
Saffron gives it its distinctive golden color.
This sport was brought to England by British officers stationed in India during the 1870s.
Players hit a feathered object over a net.
The shuttlecock can travel over 200 miles per hour during smashes.
This phenomenon was given its scientific name by Galileo in 1619.
It appears as dancing ribbons of light in polar regions.
It's also known as the Northern Lights.
This structure was originally built as a Hindu temple before becoming Buddhist.
It's the largest religious monument in the world by area.
It appears on Cambodia's national flag.
This communication method was developed in the 1830s.
It represents letters and numbers through combinations of short and long signals.
SOS is its most famous distress signal using three dots, three dashes, three dots.
This person spent over two decades traveling through Asia in the 13th century.
They served in an Asian imperial court for many years.
Their travel accounts introduced Europeans to Central Asian and Chinese cultures.
This game involves arranging geometric pieces.
It was created by a Soviet programmer during the 1980s.
It's one of the best-selling video games of all time.
This dish is traditionally served cold during hot weather.
It originated in the Andalusian region of southern Spain.
It's a chilled tomato-based soup with vegetables.
This children's game has been played in various forms for centuries.
Players navigate through numbered sections by jumping on one foot.
A marker is tossed onto numbered squares drawn on the ground.
This phenomenon forms when water saturates certain material.
It becomes more dangerous when people struggle or make sudden movements.
Despite myths, humans cannot be completely swallowed by this mixture.
This fortified complex was constructed during the medieval period.
It features intricate Islamic geometric patterns and arabesques.
It's a palace and fortress in Granada, Spain.
This material has been used for thousands of years in construction.
It hardens through a chemical reaction with water.
Modern construction uses it mixed with steel reinforcement.
This person studied specimens collected during an extended sea voyage.
They proposed a theory explaining how species change over time.
Their book On the Origin of Species revolutionized biology.
This animated series has spawned numerous spin-offs and films since its debut.
It follows the adventures of a group of teenagers and their pet.
This show features a Great Dane character who is afraid of ghosts.
This dish requires a specific cooking technique to achieve proper creaminess.
It originated in northern Italy during the Renaissance period.
It's made with short-grain rice cooked slowly with broth.
This activity requires players to propel objects across a playing surface.
It became popular on cruise ships in the 1920s.
Players push weighted pucks toward a triangular scoring area.
These groupings have been recognized and named by cultures throughout history.
Different civilizations identified different versions of these in the night sky.
The International Astronomical Union recognizes 88 of these star patterns.
This complex contains multiple palaces and cathedrals.
Its distinctive towers feature red stars installed in 1937.
It's the official residence of the Russian president in Moscow.
This process transforms a natural material into something more durable.
It involves heating material with sulfur to create cross-links.
Charles Goodyear developed this method for treating rubber.
This physician founded a therapeutic approach based on talk therapy.
They developed theories about dreams and childhood experiences.
They introduced concepts like id, ego, and superego.
This game became popular during the economic hardships of the 1930s.
Players move around a board collecting money from other players.
Boardwalk and Park Place are its most expensive properties.
This food item can be prepared with sweet or savory flavors.
It originated in northwestern France centuries ago.
It is a thin pancake that is folded around its filling
This sport is one of the oldest competitive activities in human history.
Competitors use grappling techniques to control their opponent.
It has both freestyle and Greco-Roman styles in the Olympics.
This process alters a person's state of consciousness.
Franz Mesmer was an early practitioner of this technique.
It is often used on volunteers during a magic show.
This building served as a royal palace before its current purpose.
A glass pyramid entrance was added to it in 1989.
It is the world's largest art museum.
This system encodes information in a pattern that machines can read.
It was first used commercially in the mid-1970s.
Scanners read this system's patterns to retrieve product information.
This ancient Greek philosopher taught through dialogue and questioning.
They developed a method of inquiry to stimulate critical thinking.
They were sentenced to death by drinking hemlock in 399 BCE.
This toy became a global phenomenon in the early 1980s.
It has over 43 quintillion possible configurations.
The goal is to align all colored squares on each face of this puzzle.
This one-pot dish has Spanish and French colonial influences.
It combines rice with meat, vegetables, and Creole spices.
It's a signature dish of Louisiana Cajun cuisine.
This object has been used for hunting and warfare for thousands of years.
Athletes throw it for distance in competitions.
It's a spear that competitors hurl after a running approach.
This preservation method has been practiced by multiple ancient cultures.
This process involves removing organs and drying the body.
Ancient Egyptians wrapped these preserved bodies in linen.
This religious building was constructed in the late 15th century.
This building's ceiling was painted by Michelangelo between 1508 and 1512.
It's where papal conclaves elect new popes in Vatican City.
This medical instrument was invented in the early 19th century.
It allows physicians to listen to internal sounds of the body.
Doctors use it to hear heartbeats, breathing, and other body sounds.
This scientist made a breakthrough discovery by noticing unexpected changes in their experiments.
They observed that contamination had killed surrounding bacteria.
Their 1928 discovery of penicillin revolutionized medicine.
